news.summary: Stablecoin attestation reports are third-party verifications ensuring each token is backed by real-world assets, helping build trust in the digital asset ecosystem. Attestations are point-in-time checks, not deep audits, done by independent firms like Deloitte for USDC and help assess reserves and compliance. The AICPA introduced the 2025 Criteria for Stablecoin Reporting to standardize disclosures. USDC's February 2025 attestation by Deloitte showed reserves exceeding circulating supply, backed by US Treasury securities, repurchase agreements, and cash. Verification process includes reviewing financial records and cross-checking reserves with documentation. Consequences for inadequate reserves include regulatory issues, loss of peg, and market sell-offs. Limitations of attestation reports include being snapshot-based, lack of forward-looking guarantees, and limited operational insights. Knowing how to interpret attestation reports is crucial for all participants in the crypto economy for informed decision-making and navigating risks responsibly.
news.headline: Stablecoin attestation verifies asset backing, not a full audit. USDC leads with third-party reports, essential for trust. Industry benchmark for transparent reserve reporting and compliance with MiCA regulations. Read more on understanding and importance.

news.summary: Analysts have detected large transfers of OM tokens by major Mantra investors just before the collapse of the OM token, prompting concerns about potential insider trading and project stability. Laser Digital, a key investor in Mantra, reportedly sold off a significant amount of OM tokens before the crash, with 17 wallets linked to Laser Digital moving a total of 43.6 million OM tokens (worth $227 million at the time) to exchanges. One Laser Digital-linked wallet sent 6.5 million OM tokens ($41.6 million) to OKX, while another transferred 2.2 million OM ($13 million) to Binance. The firm had received a substantial amount of OM tokens from GSR in 2023. Despite this, Laser Digital denied any involvement in the price collapse, claiming that the wallets in question did not belong to them. Another investor, Shane Shin of Shorooq Partners, received 2 million OM tokens before the crash, originating from a dormant wallet that received 2.75 million OM in April 2024. Both Laser Digital and Shorooq were part of the $109 million Mantra Ecosystem Fund but claim they did not sell any OM tokens before or during the crash. Binance attributed the collapse to "cross-exchange liquidations," while OKX flagged suspicious activity and noted changes in Mantra's tokenomics. The incident has raised concerns about potential misconduct within the project and its impact on investors.
news.headline: Mantra investors deny predumping OM token pre-crash despite transferring 43.6M tokens ($227M) to exchanges. Laser Digital, a major investor in Mantra, reportedly moved large portions of OM tokens before the cryptocurrency's collapse on April 13, with 17 wallets transferring a combined 43.6 million OM tokens valued at about $227 million to exchanges. Laser Digital has denied involvement in the OM price collapse, stating that the wallets in question did not belong to them. Another investor, Shane Shin of Shorooq Partners, received 2 million OM tokens just before the crash. Binance attributed the collapse to "cross-exchange liquidations." OKX raised concerns over Mantra's tokenomics changes and suspicious activity on multiple exchanges.

news.summary: The latest figures reveal that the global economy is expected to grow by 4.9% in 2021, marking a significant increase from the 3.2% contraction experienced in 2020. The United States economy is projected to expand by 6.4%, while the eurozone is anticipated to grow by 4.3%. In China, growth is forecasted at 8.4%. These positive trends are attributed to successful vaccination campaigns and government stimulus packages aimed at boosting economic recovery. However, uncertainties surrounding new COVID-19 variants and potential supply chain disruptions pose risks to this optimistic outlook. Central banks and policymakers continue to closely monitor the situation to ensure stability and growth in the global economy.
news.headline: Chinese gold trading surges amid rising US-China tensions.

news.summary: In April 2025, Avicanna Inc. reported its successful year in 2024, with a revenue of $25.5 million, showing a 52% increase from 2023. The company achieved a consolidated gross margin of 51%, resulting in gross profits of $12.9 million, a 94% increase from the previous year. The adjusted EBITDA improved by 68%, narrowing the loss to $1.4 million in 2024. Avicanna also repaid a $1.3 million debt and saw advancements in its commercial, R&D, and clinical programs. The company initiated a Medical Cannabis Real World Evidence Study aiming to enroll 1,000 patients. They finished the year with 42 commercial SKUs, 136 unique commercial listings, and approximately 200,000 units sold in 2024, an 8% increase from 2023. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Avicanna Inc. reports $25.5M revenue in 2024, a 52% increase from 2023, with $12.9M gross profit and 51% gross margin. CEO emphasizes financial strengthening and continued growth prospects. EBITDA loss narrowed to $1.4M, debt repayment of $1.3M. Medical cannabis Real World Evidence Study initiated in Canada. 200,000 units sold in 2024.

news.summary: Leading up to the sharp collapse of the OM token on April 13, blockchain analysts have identified large-scale token transfers by major Mantra investors, raising concerns about insider activity and project stability. Laser Digital, a strategic investor, reportedly cashed out large portions of OM tokens before the collapse, with at least two wallets linked to Laser Digital moving a combined 43.6 million OM tokens worth approximately $227 million to exchanges. Another wallet linked to Laser Digital moved about 6.5 million OM tokens ($41.6 million) to OKX and 2.2 million OM tokens ($13 million) to Binance. The firm denied involvement in the volatility, stating the referenced wallets did not belong to them. Shane Shin, a founding partner of Shorooq Partners and an investor in the Mantra Ecosystem Fund (MEF), received 2 million OM tokens from a previously dormant wallet hours before the crash. The OM collapse was attributed to "cross-exchange liquidations," with OKX and Binance addressing the issue directly. Binance suggested the crash resulted from such liquidations, while OKX Founder labeled it a "big scandal." Shorooq clarified they have not sold OM tokens and are focused on the project's long-term growth. Mantra's response to the collapse and its implications for the MEF were not available at the time of publication.
news.headline: Investors dumped Mantra's OM token before it crashed 90%- $227 million worth tokens moved; insider activity suspected.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is on the rise, with sales reaching approximately 2.1 million in 2020. This represents a significant increase of 39% compared to the previous year. The surge in EV sales can be attributed to various factors, including government incentives, advancements in technology, and growing concerns about climate change. China led the global EV market with sales of around 1.25 million vehicles, followed by Europe with 590,000 vehicles. The data also shows that the United States saw a notable increase in EV sales, reaching approximately 330,000 vehicles in 2020. Experts predict that this trend will continue as more countries commit to phasing out internal combustion engine vehicles in the coming years.
news.headline: Tech analysts face uncertainty due to profit forecast volatility, creating risk as they adopt a wait-and-see approach.
